title: artemisia dubia f. grata
intro: Artemisia dubia f. grata, also known as sweet wormwood or sweet sagewort, is a variant of the wormwood plant species, characterized by its sweet and slightly bitter flavor. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of artemisinin, a sesquiterpene lactone compound. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate fever, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, sweet wormwood is often used as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies for treating various health conditions.
